methodsAttempted.query: site:sorbonne-universite.fr Sorbonne Universite intelligence artificielle generative etudiants enseignants plagiat examen
methodsAttempted.notes: Initial official-domain search surfaced faculty and regulatory PDF material mentioning plagiarism, AI, and assessments.
French generative-AI query found official event, training, CAPSULE, and Faculty of Health results; only source-specific policy/guidance pages were retained.
Search located the official CFVU regulatory-act PDF containing the university-wide 2024-2025 Plagiat, IA et fraude assessment language.
Official Faculty of Health PDF was retained only for faculty-scoped research recommendations.

artifacts.summary: Official Sorbonne University sources were searched in French and English. University-wide assessment evidence was found in the CFVU 2024-2025 regulatory PDF; faculty-scoped research recommendations were found in the Faculty of Health PDF; CAPSULE provided an official tool-resource page for Compilatio Magister+. Event pages, continuing education offers, and commentary/resource listings were rejected as non-policy evidence.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Commission de la formation et de la vie universitaire du conseil academique du 20 juin 2024 - Modalites de controle des connaissances 2024-2025
artifacts.verificationNotes: Official actesreglementaires.sorbonne-universite.fr PDF; the text says the 2024-2025 additions apply to all formations and includes a Plagiat, IA et fraude section.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Recommandations d'utilisation de l'intelligence artificielle generative dans le cadre de la recherche
artifacts.verificationNotes: Official Faculty of Health PDF with research-use recommendations; claims are scoped to the Faculty of Health commission rather than university-wide requirements.
Official CAPSULE resource page lists Compilatio Magister+ with CAS SU access for plagiarism and AI-generated-content detection; used only for a narrow tool-availability claim.
Official event page about generative AI and disinformation, not an institutional policy or guidance source for student/research AI use.

artifacts.sourceTitle: De ChatGPT, Mistral a DeepSeek : les IA generatives demystifiees
artifacts.verificationNotes: Official continuing-education course offer about generative AI, not policy evidence for institutional AI-use rules.
Official news/commentary article about ChatGPT; not used as evidence for institutional rules.
Official-looking pedagogy resource listing was not used because the retained snippets did not provide direct institutional policy requirements.

artifacts.notes: Rejected because the page is an event announcement and does not contain direct student, teaching, research, privacy, or assessment policy language.
Rejected because it is a continuing-education offer about generative AI, not an institutional AI-use policy or guidance document.
Rejected because it is an official news/commentary article and was not used as policy evidence.
Rejected for claim support because the source discovery result suggested a resource listing rather than directly applicable policy wording.

artifacts.claimText: Sorbonne University's 2024-2025 assessment rules state that assessment documents must be the student's or assessed group's personal work, AI use is refused unless explicitly authorized, and authorized AI use should mention the source.

citation.citationTitle: Sorbonne University AI Policy Tracker record
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Commission de la formation et de la vie universitaire du conseil academique du 20 juin 2024 - Modalites de controle des connaissances 2024-2025
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: L’ensemble des documents produits dans le cadre d’une évaluation doit être l’œuvre personnelle de l’étudiant, de l’étudiante ou du groupe évalué. Lorsque le recours à l’intelligence artificielle (IA) n’est pas explicitement autorisé, il est refusé. Lorsque l’IA est autorisée, il convient de mentionner la source.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: All documents produced for an assessment must be the student's or group's personal work; AI use is refused unless explicitly authorized, and authorized AI use should mention the source.
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.notes: Approved as a narrow assessment-rules claim directly supported by the CFVU Plagiat, IA et fraude wording.

artifacts.claimText: Sorbonne University's 2024-2025 assessment rules treat unauthorized AI-generated work presented as one's own, or authorized AI use without source mention, as plagiarism.

citation.citationTitle: Sorbonne University AI Policy Tracker record
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Commission de la formation et de la vie universitaire du conseil academique du 20 juin 2024 - Modalites de controle des connaissances 2024-2025
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: De même, utiliser l’IA afin de produire des résultats en les donnant pour siens sans y être autorisé, ou l’utiliser en y étant autorisé, mais sans mentionner la source, sera considéré comme relevant du plagiat.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: Using AI to produce results as one's own without authorization, or using it with authorization but without mentioning the source, is considered plagiarism.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Recommandations d'utilisation de l'intelligence artificielle generative dans le cadre de la recherche
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Ne pas transmettre à un outil d’IA générative de contenu qui ne soit pas public ou déjà publié. Ne pas soumettre à un outil d’IA générative des données personnelles, confidentielles ou sensibles.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: Do not transmit content that is not public or already published to a generative AI tool; do not submit personal, confidential, or sensitive data to a generative AI tool.
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: Sorbonne University's Faculty of Health research recommendations say users should be transparent about generative AI use in research projects and document use when writing scientific documents such as protocols, analysis plans, reports, articles, presentations, dissertations, or theses.

citation.citationTitle: Sorbonne University AI Policy Tracker record
citation.publisher: Faculte de Sante de Sorbonne Universite

artifacts.sourceTitle: Recommandations d'utilisation de l'intelligence artificielle generative dans le cadre de la recherche
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Faire preuve de transparence sur l’utilisation d’outils IA générative dans les projets de recherche. Lors de la rédaction de documents scientifiques, tels qu’un protocole de recherche, un plan d’analyse, un rapport, un article, une présentation en congrès, un mémoire, ou une thèse, vous devez documenter l’utilisation de ces outils.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: Be transparent about the use of generative AI tools in research projects, and document the use of these tools when writing scientific documents.

artifacts.claimText: Sorbonne University's Faculty of Health research recommendations distinguish translation-only software such as DeepL or Linguee from generative AI tools and say using generative AI tools such as ChatGPT for translation is not recommended.

citation.citationTitle: Sorbonne University AI Policy Tracker record
citation.publisher: Faculte de Sante de Sorbonne Universite
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Recommandations d'utilisation de l'intelligence artificielle generative dans le cadre de la recherche
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Les logiciels à des fins exclusives de traduction (tels que DeePL ou Linguee) ne sont pas des outils d’IA générative et ne sont donc pas concernés par ces recommandations. À l’opposé, utiliser des outils d’IA générative (tel que ChatGPT) pour la traduction n’est pas recommandé.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: Translation-only software such as DeepL or Linguee is not treated as generative AI, while using generative AI tools such as ChatGPT for translation is not recommended.
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.title: Sorbonne University staged AI policy source review
artifacts.summary: Staged claim candidates for Sorbonne University are based on three official sources: the CFVU 2024-2025 assessment rules PDF, the Faculty of Health generative-AI-in-research recommendations PDF, and the CAPSULE Compilatio Magister+ resource page. Claims are not canonical and remain agent-reviewed candidates.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
Faculty of Health research recommendations are scoped to that faculty and are not presented as university-wide binding policy.
Event pages, continuing education course pages, and official AI commentary were excluded from claim evidence because they did not provide direct policy wording.
No login-only CAS destinations, external vendor pages, or non-official sources were crawled for claim evidence.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
